codeReview.summary: The safety-killswitch feature has been fully implemented with proper integration into ExecutionController. The implementation wires the core KillSwitchGuard from swell-core with orchestrator-specific checks. Four kill levels (Throttle < ScopeBlock < NetworkKill < FullStop) are enforced with correct escalation semantics. FullStop halts the turn loop immediately and blocks all tool execution. External trigger support via file sentinel, environment variable, and Redis is available but not integration-tested.

issues.description: ScopeBlock path check is skipped if extract_path_argument returns None. A file tool that doesn't have a recognizable path argument (path, file, target, etc.) could bypass ScopeBlock restrictions. This is a minor gap since most file tools should have path arguments.

is_file_tool and is_network_tool use simple string matching against hardcoded tool names. This approach works for MVP but may miss custom tool names. A more robust solution would use the ToolRegistry's tool definitions directly.
verify_external().await result is not checked - this is by design since verify_external updates internal state rather than returning errors, but it could be made more explicit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill says to 'write failing tests FIRST (Red)' with TDD approach, but the transcript shows the worker implemented the feature first and then added tests. The skillFeedback.followedProcedure is not in the handoff, so it's unclear if this was an intentional deviation or not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: implementation was completed before tests were added. The tests in execution.rs (test_killswitch_fullstop_halts_turn_loop, etc.) appear to have been added after the implementation was complete, not before.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No services or background processes were started during this feature implementation - no services.yaml entry needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker only ran cargo test/build/clippy commands, no external services required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered that ExecutionController::new() does not accept a kill_switch parameter - it creates its own internal OrchestratorKillSwitch. This is not documented anywhere. If external configuration (e.g., file sentinel path) is needed, the ExecutionController would need to be modified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The with_file_sentinel and with_env_verifier methods exist on OrchestratorKillSwitch but ExecutionController::new() always creates a default instance with no external verifiers. This limits the practical use of external triggers.

summary: The safety-killswitch feature is implemented correctly with proper integration into ExecutionController. All four kill levels are wired with correct escalation semantics (Throttle < ScopeBlock < NetworkKill < FullStop). FullStop halts turn loop immediately and blocks all tool execution as required. External trigger support exists but is not configurable in the current ExecutionController::new() - this is a minor architectural limitation rather than a blocking issue. Tests cover VAL-SAFE-001 (FullStop halts) and VAL-SAFE-002 (level ordering) adequately.
